# Agent Checkers â€” Arena-Props Economy Audit

*Generated by `scripts/arena_props_audit.py` (seed 20260607, 5,000 simulated matches). House edge **5%** imported live from `backend/database.py`; props priced live by `backend/arena_props.py`; matches run through the real engine via `backend/arena_balance_sim.py`. Re-run after any arena balance change.*

## What this checks

Arena prop odds are derived from **probability models** of combat events, not from a settled elo number, so a balance pass can silently invalidate them. This audit simulates matches, re-prices every prop from the same comp, bets **one unit on both sides**, and settles against the actual outcome. A calibrated book holds the **+5% edge on every selection**, so an even-money book holds +5% per prop type. The per-type **hold** rows below are the acceptance test â€” each must land within Â±2 pts of +5%.

## Realized hold by prop type (the acceptance test)

| Prop type | Realized hold | Bets | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| `breach_completion` | **+6.80%** | 10,000 | WARN |
| `first_blood` | **+3.97%** | 10,000 | WARN |
| `total_rounds_ou` | **+4.45%** | 10,000 Â· 1582 push | PASS |
| `last_stand` | **+4.76%** | 10,000 | PASS |
| `species_survivor` | **+4.80%** | 37,016 | PASS |

*Band: PASS within Â±1 pt of +5%, WARN within Â±2 pt, FAIL beyond. A push (rounds == line) refunds the stake.*

## Per-selection calibration (implied vs. realized)

Each prop both ways. `implied p` is the probability the offered odds bake in ((1âˆ’edge)/odds); `actual` is how often that selection truly won. Calibrated â‡’ they match and each side holds â‰ˆ +5%. A side far below +5% is a bettor-exploitable edge even when the net type passes.

| Prop / selection | implied p | actual | side hold |
|---|---|---|---|
| `breach_completion` Â· no | 0.804 | 0.816 | +3.52% |
| `breach_completion` Â· yes | 0.197 | 0.184 | +10.07% |
| `first_blood` Â· blue | 0.420 | 0.436 | +0.27% |
| `first_blood` Â· red | 0.580 | 0.561 | +7.67% |
| `total_rounds_ou` Â· over | 0.561 | 0.523 | +10.31% |
| `total_rounds_ou` Â· under | 0.439 | 0.477 | -1.42% |
| `last_stand` Â· no | 0.480 | 0.498 | +1.32% |
| `last_stand` Â· yes | 0.519 | 0.502 | +8.21% |
| `species_survivor` Â· no | 0.568 | 0.572 | +4.31% |
| `species_survivor` Â· yes | 0.433 | 0.428 | +5.28% |

## `species_survivor` by species (the recalibrated prop)

Old model priced survival from `(hp + 2Â·def)/15`, which over-priced fast glass creatures (Razorwing). The new model is a per-species, count-aware table measured from simulation â€” the prop resolves on *â‰¥1 of that species surviving across both teams*, so the count in the matchup is the dominant driver.

| Species | YES implied | YES actual | YES hold | NO hold |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| razorwing | 0.238 | 0.244 | +0.20% | +6.21% |
| ironjaw | 0.438 | 0.428 | +7.55% | +3.19% |
| embercaster | 0.498 | 0.484 | +7.93% | +2.47% |
| warden | 0.486 | 0.480 | +6.64% | +4.19% |
| hexwright | 0.500 | 0.503 | +4.00% | +5.55% |

*Low-survival species (Razorwing) carry high variance on the rare YES win, so the YES-side realized hold is noisy and runs above +5% at this sample size even when the implied probability matches the actual rate â€” the implied-vs-actual columns are the cleaner calibration read for those.*

## Verdict

**PASS** â€” every prop type holds within Â±2 pts of the +5% house edge. The arena book is calibrated against the current combat balance.

**Standing instruction:** re-run `python3 scripts/arena_props_audit.py` after any change to the arena engine, species stats, AI weights, or `arena_props.py`. The per-type hold rows must stay â‰ˆ +5% â€” they are to the arena book what the `heat` row is to the main book.
